hawkish_phrases: Inflation remains close to our two per cent medium-term target and our assessment of the inflation outlook is broadly unchanged
credit standards for business loans tightened moderately in the third quarter
inflation could turn out to be higher if a fragmentation of global supply chains pushed up import prices
A boost in defence and infrastructure spending could also raise inflation over the medium term
services inflation ticked up from 3.1 per cent to 3.2 per cent
dovish_phrases: our past interest rate cuts remain important sources of resilience
A stronger euro could bring inflation down further than expected
inflation could turn out to be lower if higher tariffs lead to lower demand for euro area exports
labour costs are set to moderate further owing to rising productivity and an easing in wage growth
forward-looking indicators, such as the ECB's wage tracker and surveys on wage expectations, point to slower wage growth
summary: The Governing Council unanimously held all three key rates unchanged with inflation close to target and a genuinely balanced assessment, maintaining strict meeting-by-meeting data dependence without pre-committing to any rate path.
